item.title: A retired lawyer in the U.S. was watching the news when he saw the story about the new Trump commemorative coin and something immediately didn't sit right with him.
item.description: A federal law passed in 1866 that explicitly prohibits living people from appearing on U.S. currency. It's not a grey area. It's not open to interpretation. It's been sitting in the books for over 150 years. The last time this actually happened was 1926 when a coin featuring Calvin Coolidge was minted while he was still alive and serving as president. The backlash was immediate. The coins were pulled. And the law was reaffirmed... Now this retired lawyer has filed a federal lawsuit against the U.S. Mint not because of who is on the coin, but because the law says it simply cannot be done. Full stop... No political agenda. No protest. Just one guy, a dusty legal statute, and a federal case that nobody in Washington apparently saw coming...
item.dc:creator: LunaAtlas

item.title: Jared Golden is today’s villain in the congressional rotation
item.description:
#1
https://www.boughtbyzionism.org/jared_golden Representative Jared Golden (D-ME) Bought for the Total Amount of: $ 817,681 Representative Jared Golden of Maine has been a steadfast supporter of Israel, a stance that aligns closely with the $817,681 he has received from AIPAC. This significant sum has clearly influenced his voting record, including his opposition to both the ceasefire resolution and the McCollum Palestinian Rights Resolution. Golden has consistently voted in favor of pro-Israel measures, including sending $41 billion in unconditional military aid to Israel while it continues its genocidal campaign against Gaza. According to the Lancet, it is estimated that 180,000 Gazans will die as a result of this ongoing genocide. Golden’s financial ties to AIPAC reveal a troubling connection between his political actions at the expense of Palestinian lives. Golden’s unwavering support for Israel extends to his public statements, including his praise of Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu after the war criminal addressed Congress. Golden stated, “I appreciated Netanyahu’s recognition of the Trump administration’s work to pass the Abraham Accords and of the Biden administration’s strong support for Israel after the deadliest attack against Jews since the Holocaust.” This statement whitewashes the brutal Israeli military campaign in Gaza, which has targeted civilian infrastructure, hospitals, and homes. Golden’s subsequent remark, “I pray for their return and the return of all the hostages, and for Hamas to lay down its arms so this deadly conflict can end,” ignores the fact that Israel has rejected ceasefire deals and bears all the responsibility for the ongoing devastation in Gaza. In May 2024, Golden criticized President Biden for withholding military assistance to Israel, insisting, “I think war is terrible... but I do not believe it is factually accurate to say that war crimes are being committed in Palestine at this time.” This dismissal of the widespread destruction, mass displacement, and death in Gaza—perpetrated by the Israeli military and paid for by U.S. tax dollars—demonstrates his callous disregard for the Palestinian lives lost. Golden’s votes to cut U.S. federal funding for UNRWA, the organization responsible for providing vital food, water, and medicine to Gazans, further illustrate his indifference to the suffering of the Palestinian people. His actions, backed by AIPAC funds, align him with a campaign of violence and destruction against the besieged people of Gaza.

17 Final Countdown for me, We Built This City is a close second.
18
19
20
21
22
23
24
25
26 Imgur Creator Spotlight: @PilotBuilder painted this and I think they do fantastic work. Check out their posts for a bunch more. *Nominated by @MadHatter69
27 Man Tax: Sean James Murphy
“Some luck lies in not getting what you thought you wanted but getting what you have, which once you have got it you may be smart enough to see is what you would have wanted had you known.” - Garrison Keillor So, I made a discord server. It's a relaxed environment where you can take/donate memes to the cause, play trivia twice a week, chat about lots of topics or about nothing at all. We also have Jack Box game night and are setting up movie night! Very laid back/relaxed vibe. Come hang with us. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ I'm also on the imgcat server for updates & fun: https://discord.gg/TpjD37jgJ9 https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator Unable to work, zero income. Feel free to leave me a tip. Those that have, y'all are the bee's knees. You've honestly been my lifeline. (Apparently doesn't work in firefox). For the couple people that have said I'm "scamming" people, I only get SNAP, so this money goes to things like TP, shampoo, laundry detergent, etc. ***Tag me if you see an artist/creator you like whose work isn't getting seen as much as it should be! Or if you are an artist/creator and want me to post something of yours, DM me
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator

item.title: Merry – life after the fall of Sauron
item.description:
This is a lore account on Meriadoc Brandybuck after the One Ring was destroyed.
Sauron was defeated on March 25 in year 3019 of the Third Age. Merry was after at his friend Aragorn’s coronation on May 1 and wedding to Arwen on Mid-year’s Day. To the wedding came Elrond with Elves from Rivendell as well as Celeborn and Galadriel with Elves from Lothlórien (art by the Brothers Hildebrandt).
Note: Gregorian month names are translations of the Shire calendar months, which do not match exactly with Gregorian dates; March 25 is for example our March 18.
A large following went to Edoras, capital of Rohan, to on August 10 bury King Théoden. Merry had been the esquire of Théoden and when he departed a week later Merry was gifted an ancient horn of silver covered in virtuous runes by Lady Éowyn and her brother, King Éomer (art by Miriam Ellis). Éowyn and Éomer held Merry dear, not least because in slaying the Witch-king “Éowyn had the aid of Théoden’s esquire” (The Return of the King; Appendix A; II; §38).
“`This is an heirloom of our house,’ said Éowyn. `It was made by the Dwarves, and came from the hoard of Scatha the Worm. Eorl the Young brought it from the North. He that blows it at need shall set fear in the hearts of his enemies and joy in the hearts of his friends, and they shall hear him and come to him.’” – RotK; VI; VI (art by Donato Giancola)
At Isengard on August 22 the four hobbits, Gandalf the Wizard and Elf companions said farewell to King Aragorn, Gimli and Legolas and to Treebeard the Ent. A week later they caught up to Saruman and Gríma, who Treebeard had released. Some expressed hope that Saruman would yet repent, or that he at at least now was harmless, but Gandalf had doubts; indeed, with cruel intent “Saruman turns towards the Shire” (RotK; Appendix B, art by Ted Nasmith).
September 13 Celeborn and Galadriel and their retinue went up the pass at Moria for Lothlórien. The hobbits came with Elrond to Rivendell on the 21st. They shared all their news with Bilbo and stayed to October 5 (art by J.R.R. Tolkien). With Gandalf they visited Bree but before the Shire Gandalf left them, trusting Frodo Baggins, Samwise Gamgee, Meriadoc, and Peregrin Took to handle any challenge.
They came to the Shire late on November 1 and found it occupied by human and half-orc ruffians led by Saruman. They had ruined much of the land and subdued or killed the inhabitants who would not collaborate (art by Manuel Castañón). Frodo Baggins and his companions inspired a rebellion. Merry commented that the Hobbits had been comfortable for too long and must be riled to action.
He encouraged Sam to check in on his beloved Rose Cotton. Meanwhile Merry would muster the people and he said (RotK; VI; VIII onwards, art by Thorbear); “`I am going to blow the horn of Rohan, and give them all some music they have never heard before.’ They rode back to the middle of the village. There Sam turned aside and galloped off down the lane that led south to Cotton’s. He had not gone far when he heard a sudden clear horn-call go up ringing into the sky. Far over hill and field it echoed; and so compelling was that call that Sam himself almost turned and dashed back.”
November 3 stood the Battle of Bywater. The Shire-folk shot many of the ruffians with bows or hewed them with axes (art by discogangsta): “But many of the strongest and most desperate got out on the west side, and attacked their enemies fiercely, being now more bent on killing than escaping. Several hobbits fell, and the rest were wavering, when Merry and Pippin, who were on the east side, came across and charged the ruffians. Merry himself slew the leader, a great squint-eyed brute like a huge orc. Then he drew his forces off, encircling the last remnant of the Men in a wide ring of archers.”
After they won they went to Bag End to face Saruman. Frodo would have allowed Saruman to live but he was slain by his own suffering servant Gríma, who was killed in turn by hobbit archers (art by John Wyatt). Thereafter a successful restoration of the Shire began, duirng which Sam spread nourishing soil from Lothlórien that he had been gifted by Galadriel, and 3020 became a year both fair and bountiful.
Frodo was Deputy Mayor while the soon handled “task of hunting out the last remnant of the ruffians was left to Merry and Pippin” (RotK; VI; IX, art by Michael Herring). “Merry and Pippin lived together for some time at Crickhollow, and there was much coming and going between Buckland and Bag End. The two young Travellers cut a great dash in the Shire with their songs and their tales and their finery, and their wonderful parties.”
“‘Lordly’ folk called them, meaning nothing but good; for it warmed all hearts to see them go riding by with their mail-shirts so bright and their shields so splendid, laughing and singing songs of far away; and if they were now large and magnificent, they were unchanged otherwise, unless they were indeed more fairspoken and more jovial and full of merriment than ever before” (ibid).
However, the torments in the mind and body of Frodo from the wound from a Morgul blade and from the One Ring could not be healed in Middle-earth. On September 21 in 3021 Frodo and Bilbo sailed over the Sea to the Blessed Realm, to live out their last years free from hurts. On the same ship went Gandalf, Galadriel and Elrond, and other Eldar. This event marks the end of the Third Age (art by Alan Lee).
With tears Pippin, Sam and Merry said farewell at the Grey Havens (RotK; VI; IX): “At last the three companions turned away, and never again looking back they rode slowly homewards; and they spoke no word to one another until they came back to the Shire. but each had great comfort in his friends on the long grey road. At last they rode over the downs and took the East Road, and then Merry and Pippin rode on to Buckland; and already they were singing again as they went.”
The three Fellowship members became prominent in their homeland. Samwise Gamgee was elected Mayor in year 6 of the Fouth Age, an office he held for decades, and in year 11 Peregrin as new head of the Took family inherited the title of Thain, leader of the Shire militia (art by Roger Garland). In between, in year 9, Meriadoc became head of the Brandybuck family when his father Saradoc ’Scattergold’ passed away (RotK; Appendix B onwards);
Buckland (red), east of the Brandybuck river, was settled later than the Shire (blue). The inhabitants were used to do things their own way and for local matters they heeded the head of the Brandybuck family, who was the heir of the founder of Buckland, naming him the Master. “Meriadoc, called the Magnificent. becomes Master of Buckland. Great gifts are sent to him by King Éomer and the Lady Éowyn of Ithilien.”
At an unknown date Merry married Estella Bolger (b. TA 2986), a younger sister of Fredegar ’Fatty’ Bolger. Estella is only known from a family tree, and though it does not include children we know that the pair had at least an unnamed son. It is safe to presume that they lived in Merry’s family estate of Brandy Hall (art by Devon Cady-Lee).
Under Aragorn, who ruled under the name of Elessar, emerged the Reunited Kingdom (estimated in blue) after he revived the kingdom of Arnor in the North. The Shire (yellow) lay within Arnor so in year 11 hobbits were granted representation: “King Elessar makes the Thain, the Master, and the Mayor Counsellors of the North-kingdom.”
Tolkien presented The Hobbit and The Lord of the Rings as ancient texts which he had translated for us to read, as if they were any other historic mythology in the real world. In this he referred to the so called Red Book of Westmarch as his main ’source’ since it among other texts contained the accounts of Bilbo, Frodo and Sam. But there were other ’sources’ for knowledge about this period and in that Meriadoc played an important role (The Fellowship of the Ring; Prologue; V onwards);
“In Brandy Hall there were many works dealing with Eriador and the history of Rohan. Some of these were composed or begun by Meriadoc himself, though in the Shire he was chiefly remembered for his Herblore of the Shire, and for his Reckoning of Years in which he discussed the relation of the calendars of the Shire and Bree to those of Rivendell, Gondor, and Rohan.”
"It was probably at Great Smials that The Tale of Years was put together, with the assistance of material collected by Meriadoc. Though the dates given are often conjectural, especially for the Second Age, they deserve attention. It is probable that Meriadoc obtained assistance and information from Rivendell, which he visited more than once." Note: The Tale of Years is the entry mostly labled Appendix B in the Return of the King (art by @galerifoton at https://imgur.com/gallery/8lR70w9)
Herblore of the Shire provides significant information about tobacco, called pipe-weed, found elsewhere in the prolouge, but Meriadoc also cared for linguistics: “"He also wrote a short treatise on Old Words and Names in the Shire, having special interest in discovering the kinship with the language of the Rohirrim of such ’shire-words’ as mathom and old elements in place names.”
The Great Smials was home to the Took family and there were books which were of little interest to Shire-folk, though they were more important for larger history. For Peregrin and his successors collected many manuscripts about the Dúnedain which had been written by scribes of Gondor: “Only here in the Shire were to be found extensive materials for the history of Númenor and the arising of Sauron.”
In year 61 Rosie Gamgee passed away. Then it is told that Sam, at age 102, went to the Grey Havens and he sailed over the Sea (art by Ted Nasmith). In the spring of year 63 a message came from Rohan to Buckland that King Éomer, age 94, wished to see his old friend Meriadoc once more. From what followed it is a reasonable assumption that both Merry and Pippin were already widowers;
“Meriadoc was then old (102) but still hale. He took counsel with his friend the Thain, and soon after they handed over their goods and offices to their sons and rode away over the Sam Ford, and they were not seen again in the Shire. It was heard after that Master Meriadoc came to Edoras and was with King Éomer before he died in that autumn” (art by RenjuArt).
“Then he and Thain Peregrin went to Gondor and passed what short years were left to them in that realm, until they died and were laid in Rath Dínen among the great of Gondor.” Rath Dínen was the road that passed through the Hallows, or the Tombs, behind the Minas Tirith citadel, where rested its rulers and great heroes (art by Ralph Damiani).
There in year 120 they would finally be joined by their old Ranger friend (art by Rin): “In this year on March 1st came at last the Passing of King Elessar. It is said that the beds of Meriadoc and Peregrin were set beside the bed of the great king.”
Some related accounts on lives after the fall of Sauron: Mortals in the Blessed Realm https://imgur.com/gallery/C6GSVN7 Aragorn https://imgur.com/gallery/xPBYmPy Pippin https://imgur.com/gallery/mrN9IK0 Gimli https://imgur.com/gallery/PWF7HDo Legolas https://imgur.com/gallery/YQMvvZ7
In case you enjoyed this and would like to read further Tolkien lore posts INDEX: character bios https://imgur.com/gallery/axV2mPF INDEX: folks, animals, items, etc https://imgur.com/gallery/AQK9pJL
item.dc:creator: Ngugi

item.title: RADICAL Christians worship in the White House
item.description: We'll bomb a country back to the stone age for mixing Allah into their government, then elect a guy who thinks Jesus personally endorsed his tax policy. And we'll do both things with the same flag pin on.
item.dc:creator: SofisticatedPhalcon
